messages.content:
Pop was originally launched on 29 May 2003 by Chart Show Channels (CSC) Media Group as Toons&Tunes. Toons&Tunes was then later rebranded as its current name, Pop (formerly stylized as pop and POP!) in the following month.

In the channel’s early years, links were presented by Rorry, a lime green dragon with a Scottish accent, who was animated live. He was accompanied by Purrdy, a dragon/cat hybrid who also appeared in the original Tricky programme on ITV1, it was sometimes followed by a programme known as “The Cheeky Monkey Show” which features the live links of the animated coloured monkeys known as “The Cheeky Monkeys,” that was later known by appearing on its sister channel Tiny Pop until its retirement at the end of June 2009.

Originally, the network focused on music videos with animated programming also part of the schedule, but with the numerous music video network options on British television at the time, this focus was quickly abandoned in 2004, when the animated content became much more prominent. Music videos continued to be carried on and off until 2010, before eventually being abandoned entirely.[citation needed, In 2010, Pop had several popular shows, such as The Smurfs, GoGoRiki, Oggy and the Cockroaches, etc.

In 2012, Pop introduces semi-mascots known as the MoonGoons, These are animal-like aliens, with the 2 main ones being a blue squid named Boofus and an orange rabbit named Diggle, These were gone by the time of the channel’s next rebrand in 2015, Also in 2014 CSC Media Group had folded into Sony.

On 1 September 2020, Pop rebranded its on-air identity, with the premiere of Bakugan: Armored Alliance. And on the same day, starting with Pokémon Journeys: The Series, the network became the new British home of the Pokémon anime, moving from its previous broadcasters, ITV1 and CITV, of over 20 years. The channel was also sold by Sony to Narrative Entertainment

On 9 November 2022, Narrative Entertainment announced the launch of a new FAST channel, Pop Kids, to stream on Samsung TV Plus, the new FAST network airs shows from Pop and Tiny Pop.

Sometime in 2022, Pop began to branch into YouTube content, as they approached Joe Tasker for a new show called Swipe It! With Joe Taker, they would later acquire from content creators to use on their channel, including LankyBox, Denis and EthanGamer (the latter exclusively airing on Pop Player).

By 2023 Alvin & the Chipmunks, Squish, Miraculous Ladybug and Pokemon were among the channels popular shows,

Write a fictional continuation, beginning with another rebrand in January 2024, coinciding with Xiaolin Showdown being added

In January 2024, Pop underwent another significant rebrand, marking a new era for the popular children's channel. The rebranding was not just in the visual identity but also in the programming strategy, aiming to blend nostalgia with the new, to appeal both to children and the adults who grew up watching some of the older shows. With this rebrand, Pop announced the premiere of "Xiaolin Showdown," a beloved early 2000s animated series, integrating it into their weekend morning lineup. 

To celebrate the rebrand and the addition of "Xiaolin Showdown," Pop launched a month-long "Retro Revival" campaign. This campaign included the return of several classic animated shows from the late 90s and early 2000s, such as "Code Lyoko" and "Samurai Jack," which were interspersed with their current popular shows like "Alvin & the Chipmunks" and "Miraculous Ladybug." 

The "Retro Revival" campaign was a hit, drawing in not only the target demographic of children but also adults in their late twenties to early thirties, who reveled in the nostalgia. Encouraged by this success, Pop decided to create a dedicated "Retro Weekend" slot that would air classic animated shows and cartoons.

Recognizing the power of interactive and digital content, Pop also expanded its YouTube channel and Pop Player app offerings. They introduced "Pop Throwback Thursdays," a new digital series where they uploaded episodes of classic shows and hosted live watch parties with special guests, including voice actors and show creators.

In a move to further embrace the digital age and its possibilities, Pop introduced an innovative feature in the summer of 2024 called "PopPicks." This feature, available on the Pop Player app, allowed the audience to vote for which shows they wanted to watch during special interactive programming blocks. "PopPicks" quickly became a favorite amongst viewers, nurturing a sense of community and giving them a say in the programming.

By late 2024, Pop's rebranding efforts and strategic programming decisions had reinvigorated the channel. Ratings surged, and Pop was praised for its blend of new content and cherished classics, its embrace of digital interactivity, and its efforts to cater to a wide audience range, from current kids to those who were kids at the heart. The channel announced plans to explore original content production, focusing on creating shows that would inspire, entertain, and potentially become the beloved classics of the future.
Write a tweet with some shows that were added back to Pop

🌟Nostalgia Alert!🌈 #PopTV's Retro Revival is here & it's EPIC! Catch the return of 🌀"Xiaolin Showdown,"🤖"Code Lyoko,"👹"Samurai Jack," & more on our Retro Weekend slot! Time to relive those childhood mornings 📺✨ #RetroRevival #ClassicCartoons #MemoryLane @PopTV
Write a forum convo about the "Forgotten", shows that Pop aired in the early 2010s, but are not acknowledging at all

**Thread Title: The "Forgotten" Gems of Pop - Early 2010s Shows Discussion**

---

**CerealBeforeMilk22**:
Hey everyone! Been thinking about some of the shows Pop used to air back in the early 2010s. Shows like **"GoGoRiki"** and **"Oggy and the Cockroaches"**. It's like they've vanished into thin air with hardly any mention or rerun on the channel nowadays. Does anyone else remember these or have others to add to the "forgotten" list? 

---

**SaturdayMorningNostalgia**:
Absolutely! What about **"Cosmic Quantum Ray"**? That show was quirky and had some great educational content without being too in-your-face about it. It seems like channels often move forward without acknowledging these classics. Would love to see them do a throwback segment.

---

**ToonBingeQueen**:
Yes! And don't get me started on **"The Smurfs."** While not originally from the 2010s, Pop gave it a platform for a whole new generation. Now, it's like they never aired it. A throwback segment would be amazing.

---

**VintageToonFan**:
**"Hero 108"** anyone? That show was bonkers but in the best way. It's interesting how channels evolve and leave behind certain shows as they rebrand or shift focus. Pop has moved towards newer titles, but a nod to their eclectic past offerings would be nice.

---

**PixelPals**:
It's all about the ratings and current content demand, I guess. But acknowledging or rerunning these "forgotten" shows, even if just on their digital platforms or a dedicated time slot, could tap into the nostalgia market. They're missing a trick here!

---

**RorrysRevenge**:
You're all spot on! It's a shame. Shows like **"The Cheeky Monkeys"** segments and even using those semi-mascots, the MoonGoons, gave the channel such a unique charm. Bringing some of that charm back, even in small doses, could really differentiate Pop in today's saturated kids' TV market.

---

**NerdCultureCritic**:
It's a delicate balance for networks, I imagine. Keeping relevant with the newer audience while also trying not to alienate older fans who grew up with their earlier content. A "Retro Revival" weekend like they recently did or dedicating space on their streaming service for these classics could satisfy both worlds.

---

**CerealBeforeMilk22**:
Thanks for the input, everyone! It's great seeing I'm not the only one who remembers and appreciates these shows. Maybe Pop or any channel really, should consider our voices for a blast from the past – it'd be a hit across generations, I bet. Who knows, maybe they'll catch on to this sentiment. Until then, let's keep our beloved "forgotten" shows alive in discussions like this! 📺❤️

---

Write a forum convo with the full current show list, and also say 2 former shows they believe are "dead" in the Pop verse and also the mascot getting the most attention, and the one basically forgotten/dead

**Thread Title: Pop's Current Show Lineup & The Forgotten Corners of Pop History**

---

**ToonArchivist**:
Hey everyone! I've compiled a list of the current shows airing on Pop as of 2023 for easy reference. Shows like **"Alvin & the Chipmunks," "Squish," "Miraculous Ladybug,"** and **"Pokemon"** are leading the pack. Pop has definitely evolved over the years, blending new with some nostalgic offerings. With that said, there are a couple of shows and mascots from the past that seem to have fallen into oblivion. Shows like **"GoGoRiki"** and **"The Cheeky Monkeys Show"** seem to be "dead" in the Pop verse. As for mascots, Boofus and Diggle (the MoonGoons) were buzzing in 2012, but now, hardly anyone remembers them. Contrastingly, Rorry got a lot of attention back in the day. Thoughts?

---

**RetroKid90**:
Wow, **ToonArchivist**, that's a solid list you've got there! It's amazing to see how Pop's lineup has shifted focus over the years. Regarding the "dead" shows, I think **"GoGoRiki"** and **"The Cheeky Monkeys Show"** likely didn't fit into the evolving brand identity or didn't hold up in terms of viewership. It's a shame, though. They had their charm. As for the mascots, Rorry was such a unique feature for Pop; it gave the channel a more personal touch. It's sad to see some become relics of the past.

---

**NostalgiaNerd101**:
I totally remember the MoonGoons! Boofus and Diggle were kind of quirky but added a fun element to the channel. It's intriguing how certain aspects of a channel's history, like these mascots, become "forgotten" as time moves on. The focus clearly shifts towards what's trending or has a broad appeal. I guess that's just the nature of the television business.

---

**SaturdaySpoon**:
Agreed, the television landscape is always evolving. The shows and mascots from the past, like **"GoGoRiki"** and the MoonGoons, serve as timestamps of the channel's history. They might be "dead" in terms of current content strategy, but they're certainly not forgotten by those of us who grew up watching them. It's a bit sad to think new generations will miss out on these quirky characters and shows unless Pop decides to do some throwback events or specials.

---

**PixelPioneer**:
It's interesting when you think about the lifecycle of TV shows and mascots. Some become iconic and remain in the loop for years, while others fade away quietly. The current lineup on Pop is pretty strong, showing they've adapted well with time. I do wish there was a way for channels to occasionally bring back or at least acknowledge their former shows and mascots. It'd be a great way to bridge generations of viewers and celebrate the channel's legacy.

---

**MemoryLaneMaster**:
You've all made some excellent points. TV channels like Pop have the challenging task of staying relevant in a rapidly changing entertainment environment. While it's understandable that not all shows or mascots can stay in the spotlight, acknowledging their past a bit more could enrich the channel's culture. Maybe they could consider introducing a "Blast from the Past" day or something similar. It would be a win-win, offering a slice of nostalgia for long-time fans and a fresh, quirky take for new viewers.

Write a follow up in 2025 where the current mascot is a talking potato, also XS is the only old toon getting healthy reruns, also a user is begging for GoGoRiki back, saying it would make a welcome change from Alvin or Squish

**Thread Title: Pop Channel Update 2025 - A Talking Potato and XS?**

---

**ToonReviver**:
Hey everyone, diving into the 2025 update on Pop's programming landscape! 📺 Seems like the channel has taken quite an interesting turn. The current mascot is now a talking potato named "Spudly." Who would've thought? Also, looks like "**XS**" is the only old-school cartoon getting healthy reruns on the channel.

However, I've noticed a bit of uniformity with shows like **"Alvin & the Chipmunks"** and **"Squish"** dominating the lineup. Don't get me wrong, they're great, but I can't help feeling the variety has taken a hit. Thoughts? 🥔✨

---

**NostalgiaSeeker**:
**Spudly** as a mascot is honestly such a quirky and bold move by Pop, and I'm here for it! 😂 As for **XS** getting reruns, it's refreshing to see them bringing some classics back. However, I do agree that their current lineup is becoming a tad predictable.

---

**GoGoFanatic**:
Honestly, I'm begging Pop to bring back **"GoGoRiki"**! With the current lineup being so saturated with shows like **"Alvin & the Chipmunks"** and **"Squish,"** adding **"GoGoRiki"** would introduce some much-needed variety. That show had such a unique charm and humor that's unmatched by anything airing right now. Please, Pop, shake things up for us! 🙏 #BringBackGoGoRiki

---

**ModernToonCritic**:
I get the sentiment behind wanting more variety like **"GoGoRiki."** The thing is, channels often rely on analytics and viewer demand, which might explain the heavy rotation of shows like Alvin and Squish. They're proven performers. Introducing Spudly and the reruns of **XS** seem to be their current strategy for shaking things up. Maybe if there's enough demand, they'll consider diversifying further?

---

**DigitalProphet**:
**Spudly the talking potato** has definitely become the face of Pop, and it's an amusing choice that seems to resonate well with the audience. The success of **XS** reruns might actually pave the way for more classic cartoons to return. It shows there's an appetite for nostalgia among viewers. Perhaps a well-orchestrated campaign for **"GoGoRiki"** could sway Pop's decision-makers. #NostalgiaPower

---

**ClassicCartoonLover**:
I've noticed the pleas for **"GoGoRiki"** and more variety. Spudly is entertaining, but the appeal of Pop has always been its diverse range of shows. With XS doing well, there's clearly a market for the classics alongside the new. It's time Pop considers more balance in their lineup. Variety is the spice of life (and television)!

---

**ToonReviver** (Thread Starter):
Great points all around! The consensus seems to be craving for variety, with a special shoutout for **"GoGoRiki."** Perhaps it's time for a formal petition or social media campaign to bring back more classics. Spudly, if you're listening, maybe you can help usher in a new era of diversity on Pop? Here's hoping 2025 brings more surprises (and variety) from Pop! 📺🥔 #MoreClassicsPlease

Write a follow up where our favourite warriors are now the 3rd most aired show on Pop (the YouTube banner literally has Omi, Raimundo and Kimiko alongside Alvin, Simon, Theodore, Squish, Pod, Peggy, Ash and Pikachu

**Thread Title: "Our Favorite Warriors" Soar to New Heights on Pop - A 2025 Spectacular Update!**

---

**WarriorFan101**:
Hey Pop enthusiasts! 🌟 Have you checked out the latest YouTube banner? It's official: "Our Favorite Warriors" featuring Omi, Raimundo, and Kimiko has skyrocketed to become the 3rd most aired show on Pop, sharing the spotlight with iconic characters like Alvin, Simon, Theodore, Squish, Pod, Peggy, Ash, and Pikachu. This is HUGE! Let's talk about this incredible rise to prominence and what it means for Pop! 📺✨

---

**RetroToonAdmirer**:
Absolutely ecstatic to see "Our Favorite Warriors" getting the recognition it deserves! It’s a testament to how timeless stories and great character development resonate with audiences, both new and old. The mix of nostalgia and modern favorites on the banner perfectly encapsulates Pop's evolving identity. Incredible move!

---

**DigitalNostalgiaPhD**:
This is fantastic news! The climb to the 3rd spot is no small feat, especially in today's competitive children's programming. Sharing banner space with the likes of Pokémon and Alvin & the Chipmunks? "Our Favorite Warriors" is truly in elite company. Does anyone have insights into why Pop decided to ramp up the airings?

---

**AnimationAnalyst**:
**@DigitalNostalgiaPhD**, great question! It likely boils down to viewer engagement and demand. "Our Favorite Warriors" has a unique blend of action, humor, and heart that appeals across age groups. Plus, with the resurgence of early 2000s nostalgia, it was the perfect time for Pop to double down on a proven favorite.

---

**PopChannelInsider**:
Can confirm that fan feedback played a huge role in this decision! Pop closely monitors social media reactions, forum discussions, and viewership metrics. The love for "Our Favorite Warriors" was overwhelming, making it a clear choice to feature prominently. It’s a win-win, reinvigorating long-time fans and introducing these amazing characters to a new generation.

---

**BringBackTheClassics**:
This is heartwarming news! "Our Favorite Warriors" holds a special place in my heart, and seeing it get such prime placement fills me with joy. It shows Pop's commitment to embracing its roots while pushing forward. Also, big kudos for integrating these characters with the newer stars on the banner; it's like bridging two eras of epic storytelling!

---

**MascotMaven**:
Speaking of characters, does anyone else think Spudly the Potato might get along famously with the "Our Favorite Warriors" crew? Imagine the crossover potential! Pop is really setting the stage for some creative and fun television.

---

**ToonReviver** (Thread Starter):
Absolutely loving this energy and enthusiasm for "Our Favorite Warriors"! Pop's strategic choices are paying off, showcasing a blend of the new wave and throwback classics. Considering the channel's current trajectory, what other classic shows do you hope make a triumphant return? Let's keep this positive momentum going and maybe influence the next big comeback! 🌈🌟 #PopRevival

Write a follow up where they wonder why it's named Our Favourite Warriors and not its real name "Xiaolin Showdown", And also, a user really wants Pogoriki, Chikoriki and the rest of the GoGoRiki gang back, but they seem to slowly be losing hope

**Thread Title: Why "Our Favorite Warriors" and Not "Xiaolin Showdown"? Plus, A Plea for the GoGoRiki Gang**

---

**MysticMoniker**:
I've been pondering why Pop is referring to "Xiaolin Showdown" as "Our Favorite Warriors." Don't get me wrong—it’s catchy, but it feels like a significant departure from the original title that many of us grew up loving. Does anyone have insights or theories on this rebranding choice? Also, can't help but notice the strong desire for the return of the GoGoRiki gang. It's tough seeing fans holding out hope. 

---

**ToonLoreKeeper**:
Interesting observation, **@MysticMoniker**. The rebranding to "Our Favorite Warriors" might be Pop's strategy to refresh the show for a new audience while tapping into nostalgia. It creates a sense of mystery and intrigue for newcomers and a fond sense of recognition for fans of the original. As for the GoGoRiki gang, I share the sentiment. Their unique charm is unmatched, and it's a shame they're fading into obscurity.

---

**NostalgiaNavigator**:
Honestly, the name change threw me off at first, but I've warmed up to "Our Favorite Warriors." It emphasizes the characters, who are the heart of the show, making it more personal and inviting. Regarding GoGoRiki, I've been rooting for Pogoriki, Chikoriki, and the gang’s return. With each passing year, hope dims a bit, but never say never in the world of animation!

---

**GoGoRikiHopeful**:
As someone who’s ardently campaigned for the return of GoGoRiki, it's disheartening to feel like the pleas are unheard. Pogoriki, Chikoriki, and all have a special place in many hearts. "Our Favorite Warriors" getting the spotlight gives me a glimmer of hope, but it's tough. The landscape has changed, and perhaps there's just not enough room for our beloved GoGoRiki crew anymore. 😞

---

**BrandNostalgiaExpert**:
The rebrand to "Our Favorite Warriors" could be a marketing goldmine for Pop, making an old classic feel like a brand-new discovery for younger viewers. It's all about presentation! As for GoGoRiki fans like **@GoGoRikiHopeful**, your passion is evident and touching. Channels evolve, but fan voices do make a difference. Keep the conversations going, engage on social media, and who knows? Surprises can happen, and revivals are always in vogue.

---

**PopRevivalist**:
Echoing **@BrandNostalgiaExpert** here—never underestimate the power of a dedicated fanbase. “Our Favorite Warriors” shows that Pop is willing to innovate with their content strategy, which gives hope to other classics making a comeback. For the GoGoRiki gang, let’s keep rallying, sharing why it matters, and perhaps start a #BringBackGoGoRiki campaign. Your enthusiasm is the spark that could light the way for their return!

---

**ToonReviver** (Thread Starter):
Thanks for the insight and support, everyone! The discussion around "Our Favorite Warriors" vs. "Xiaolin Showdown" sheds light on the complexities of brand reinvigoration. As for GoGoRiki, **@GoGoRikiHopeful** and fellow fans, your dedication is admirable. Let's use this forum as a launching pad for positive action and visibility for all the shows we miss and love. Who knows what the future holds? Let's stay hopeful and active! 🌟📺 #HopeForGoGoRiki
Write a follow up where Squish is gone, and you WON'T believe what's coming back

**Thread Title: "Squish" Bids Farewell to Pop & You WON’T Believe What’s Making a Comeback!**

---

**ToonTimeTraveler**:
Hey, Pop community! 📺✨ It's a bittersweet day; we've officially said goodbye to "Squish" on Pop. While it's sad to see it go, the BIG news is what's set to fill that slot. Brace yourselves... "GoGoRiki" is making a COMEBACK! After much rallying, wishful thinking, and nostalgia-fueled discussions – it's happening! 🎉 #GoGoRikiReturns

---

**RetroRevampRaver**:
WOW, I’m speechless! 😱 Never thought I’d see the day "GoGoRiki" makes its grand return. This is a HUGE win for nostalgia fans and a showcase of Pop’s commitment to listening to their audience's outcry for classic content. Color me excited and ready for the quirky adventures of Pogoriki, Chikoriki, and the whole gang!

---

**AnimationAppreciator**:
Unbelievable! This is proof positive that our voices and social media campaigns can truly make an impact. "GoGoRiki" coming back after the departure of "Squish" shows a fantastic balance between trying new things and honoring beloved classics. Kudos to Pop for this bold move. I'm already setting my DVR to record every episode!

---

**NostalgiaNurturer**:
As much as I adored "Squish" and am sad to see it go, the return of "GoGoRiki" is like a dream come true. This feels like a victory for every fan who flooded forums, social media, and even Pop’s inbox begging for its return. Our persistence paid off! Can't wait to dive back into the world of GoGoRiki. Pop really outdid themselves this time!

---

**GoGoGuru**:
I was starting to lose hope, but this news has reignited my passion for classic toons! "GoGoRiki" holds a special place in my cartoon-loving heart, and knowing that new generations will get to experience its unique charm is incredibly fulfilling. Massive respect to Pop for making this bold move. Let’s ensure it gets all the support it deserves!

---

**DoubtfulDave**:
Not gonna lie, I was among the skeptics who thought "GoGoRiki" would never see the light of day on Pop again. This is a pleasant surprise that’s got me eating my words. It’s refreshing to see a channel actually take fan feedback into account. Curious to see how it resonates with today’s audience. Here’s hoping it sparks a trend of reviving more forgotten gems!

---

**ToonReviver** (Thread Starter):
What an emotional rollercoaster! From saying goodbye to "Squish" to welcoming back "GoGoRiki" – Pop is truly taking us on a wild ride. It’s a testament to the power of community and nostalgia. Let’s all tune in and support "GoGoRiki"’s return, ensuring it's a success and opens the door for more beloved classics to make their way back to our screens. Here’s to new beginnings and reviving old favorites! 🌈🎉 #PopChannelMagic

Write a tweet saying the writing was on the wall for Squish when Xiaolin overtook it in ratings, GoGoRiki was brought back because the employees genuinely misses the characters, especially our blue rabbit Pogoriki

📉🚀 The writing was on the wall for #Squish when #XiaolinShowdown overtook it in ratings. The heartwarming return of #GoGoRiki wasn't just about numbers; it was driven by genuine affection from the Pop team, especially for our beloved blue rabbit, Pogoriki. Sometimes, it's more than just business—it's about bringing back the characters we truly miss. 💙🐰 #NostalgiaRevival
Write a farewell ad for Squish, One announcer says "personally I blame Omi for getting more views, But anyways, We've been thinking back to a neglected show"

🌟✨ **Farewell to Squish - A Loving Goodbye & A Look to the Past** ✨🌟

[Scene fades in with soft, nostalgic music playing. The iconic Pop logo appears on the screen, followed by a montage of heartwarming moments from "Squish."]

🎙️ **Announcer #1**: "As we bid a fond farewell to 'Squish,' a show that's brought us so much laughter and joy, we can't help but look back on the amazing journey we've shared."

[Cut to clips of "Squish" showing the funniest and most endearing moments, laughter echoing softly in the background.]

🎙️ **Announcer #2** (with a light, playful tone): "Personally, I blame Omi for snagging more views with those Xiaolin Showdowns. But hey, it's all in good fun, right?"

[A brief comedic montage of Omi in action from "Xiaolin Showdown," performing dramatic moves and smiling at the camera.]

🎙️ **Announcer #1**: "But now, as one chapter ends, it's time to revisit some old friends. We began to think back to a show that, perhaps, was a bit neglected but held a special place in our hearts."

[A nostalgic transition with the Pop logo swirls into a delightful preview of "GoGoRiki," showcasing fan-favorite moments and the ever-lovable Pogoriki.]

🎙️ **Announcer #2**: "That's right! 'GoGoRiki' is bouncing back into action! It's time to rekindle the joy with our beloved characters, especially our blue friend, Pogoriki."

[The montage ends with the Pop logo and details on when to catch "GoGoRiki."]

🎙️ **Announcer #1**: "So, join us in waving goodbye to 'Squish'—thank you for the memories. And get ready to welcome back 'GoGoRiki.' Here at Pop, it's all about the adventures that bring us together."

[Scene fades out with the heartwarming tune playing, closing on the message, "Thank You, Squish. Welcome Back, GoGoRiki!" along with the air dates.]

🎙️ **Announcer #2** (softly): "Stay tuned for more laughs, more adventures, and more memories, only here on Pop."

[End of Ad]

🌈 **#FarewellSquish #WelcomeBackGoGoRiki #PopTVAdventures** 🌈
